ddls:
CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the response was submitted */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the respondent (if provided) */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the respondent */,
  "experience_ml" TEXT /* Work experience with Machine/Deep Learning */,
  "experience_drl" TEXT /* Work experience with Deep Reinforcement Learning */,
  "programming_languages" TEXT /* Programming languages and frameworks used by the respondent */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry the respondent works in */,
  "organization_size" TEXT /* Size of the respondent's organization */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Respondent's highest level of education */,
  "drl_project_experience" TEXT /* Number of DRL projects the respondent has worked on */,
  "drl_tools_used" TEXT /* List of DRL tools the respondent has used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "problems" (
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each problem */,
  "problem_description" TEXT /* Description of the problem encountered in DRL */,
  "problem_category" TEXT /* Category of the problem */,
  "problem_subcategory" TEXT /* Subcategory of the problem */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("problem_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "problem_responses" (
  "problem_response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the problem response entry */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the response that reported the problem */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the problem identified */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the problem (e.g., minor, major) */,
  "fix_difficulty" TEXT /* Difficulty level of fixing the problem (e.g., easy, hard) */,
  "encountered_before" INTEGER /* Indicates if the respondent has encountered the problem before */,
  "problem_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of encountering the problem */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("problem_response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_problem_responses_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES responses ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_problem_responses_problem_id FOREIGN KEY ("problem_id") REFERENCES problems ("problem_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interviews" (
  "interview_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interview record */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the response associated with the interview */,
  "available" INTEGER /* Indicates if the respondent is available for an interview */,
  "interview_date" TEXT /* Scheduled date and time of the interview */,
  "interview_status" TEXT /* Status of the interview (e.g., scheduled, completed, cancelled) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interview_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interviews_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES responses ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the response associated with the comment */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Content of the comment provided by the respondent */,
  "comment_category" TEXT /* Category of the comment */,
  "comment_subcategory" TEXT /* Subcategory of the comment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES responses ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each programming language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the programming language or framework */,
  "language_type" TEXT /* Type of programming language (e.g., scripting, compiled) */,
  "language_popularity" TEXT /* Popularity of the programming language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_languages" (
  "response_language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response-language entry */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the survey response */,
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the programming language used */,
  "language_proficiency" TEXT /* Respondent's level of proficiency in the language */,
  "language_usage" TEXT /* Frequency of using the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_languages_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES responses ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_languages_language_id FOREIGN KEY ("language_id") REFERENCES languages ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fixes" (
  "fix_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fix recommendation */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the problem the fix addresses */,
  "fix_description" TEXT /* Description of the suggested fix for the problem */,
  "fix_complexity" TEXT /* Complexity of the suggested fix (e.g., easy, hard) */,
  "fix_effectiveness" TEXT /* Effectiveness of the suggested fix (e.g., high, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fix_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_fixes_problem_id FOREIGN KEY ("problem_id") REFERENCES problems ("problem_id")
)
